title: Poem of Ages
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Quote|And a knight shall come, a battle will be fought, and the prisoners shall go free.|A prophecy of the poem|<ref name="Wizard">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/kylekatarnstale5|text=Light and Dark: Kyle Katarn's Tale, Mission 5|int=Kyle Katarn's Tale}}</ref>}}
The '''Poem of Ages''' was the [[Bouncer]] collection of their entire history, including the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan]]. Toward the end of the poem was a collection of prophecies, including two that foretold the comings of [[Morgan Katarn|Morgan]] and [[Kyle Katarn]] to the [[Valley of the Jedi]].

In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], the Bouncers saw Morgan Katarn on a [[Landspeeder/Legends|floater]] moving from [[Fort Nowhere]] in the east to [[Olmondo]] to the west, and recognized him as the figure of the prophecy.<ref name="Dark Forces: Rebel Agent">''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''</ref>

Unknown to him, that night Morgan stopped his floater (the ''[[Old Codger]]'') among some dunes, that were the ruins of Olmondo. There he contacted the Bouncers. One Bouncer communicated a part of the prophecy about the expected arrival of a knight. Morgan explained that although he [[Force-sensitive/Legends|could feel the fluctuations]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], he had never been a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]. The Bouncer went on communicating that they expected an alien who, flying through the air, would come from the east and stop at Olmondo at night, seeking for the [[Valley of the Jedi|Valley]], as Morgan did travel westwards on a [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcraft]]. When Morgan insisted that he never stayed in Olmondo, the Bouncers revealed to him that its ruins were right under the sand.<ref name="Dark Forces: Rebel Agent" /><ref name="RA audio">[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]]</ref>

In order to follow the prophecy, they spent the night on Olmondo, and then Morgan asked for the directions<ref name="Dark Forces: Rebel Agent" /> although the Valley lay to the direction he was heading all along.<ref name="RA audio" />
